name: Salad of Mixed Greens (Ew)
recipeIngredient: 1/4 cup Strong brewed tea, such as oolong pekoe or Earl Grey
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
Chive blossoms and raspberries for garnish
recipeInstructions:
Note: For the salad mix called mesclun, seeds of lettuces, herbs, and leafy vegetables are combined and sown together in one garden bed. When the plants are only three to four inches high, they are harvested. Mesclun is available at specialty greengrocers. Of course, you can use your own mix of baby lettuces, such as lamb's lettuce, oak leaf, spinach greens, cress, or rocket.
1. In a small bowl, whisk together tea, oil, vinegar, and mustard until smooth.
2. Stir in chives and season with salt and pepper.
3. Toss mesclun with the vinaigrette in a large bowl.
4. Scatter chive blossoms and raspberries over the top and serve.
